Title: Placeholder maintainer identity remains in DESCRIPTION
Evidence: DESCRIPTION uses maintainers@example.com for Author/Maintainer metadata.
Impact: Release metadata is not publication-ready and weakens package ownership traceability.
Recommended_next_action: Replace placeholder maintainer identity with a real maintainer record before stabilization.

Title: DESCRIPTION is missing URL
Impact: Repository and project-home discovery are weaker than expected for release-grade metadata.
Recommended_next_action: Add canonical repository or project URLs to DESCRIPTION.

Title: DESCRIPTION is missing BugReports
Impact: Users and reviewers lack a declared issue-reporting endpoint.
Recommended_next_action: Add a stable issue tracker URL to DESCRIPTION.

Title: Repository has no top-level README
Evidence: No README* file was found at the package root.
Impact: New users and reviewers lack a primary package overview and install/use guidance.
Recommended_next_action: Add a root README that describes purpose, installation, and core usage patterns.

Title: Repository has no top-level NEWS file
Evidence: No NEWS* file was found at the package root.
Impact: Change tracking is less auditable during stabilization and release preparation.
Recommended_next_action: Add a NEWS or NEWS.md file for release-facing change history.

Title: No vignettes are present
Evidence: The vignettes/ directory is absent or contains no vignette sources.
Impact: Long-form usage guidance is missing for a metrics package with multiple wrappers and compatibility behaviors.
Recommended_next_action: Add at least one vignette or explicitly document why vignette coverage is intentionally absent.

Title: Compatibility tracker overstates the current public export surface
Evidence: COMPATIBILITY_TRACKER.md marks hydroGOF-style items as implemented, but 33 tracked names are not exported via NAMESPACE: KGE, KGEkm, KGElf, KGEnp, NSE, R2, VE, br2
Impact: API compatibility claims are difficult to audit because documentation and namespace evidence disagree.
Recommended_next_action: Reconcile compatibility docs with actual exports, or document which items are method names rather than exported functions.

Title: No coverage automation or config was detected
Evidence: No coverage workflow, codecov config, or covr-specific repository file was found.
Impact: Coverage blind spots are harder to locate before stabilization work begins.
Recommended_next_action: Add a coverage command or workflow and record expected thresholds separately from this audit.

Title: No lint automation or config was detected
Evidence: No lint workflow and no .lintr config were found.
Impact: Style and static-analysis regressions may accumulate unnoticed.
Recommended_next_action: Add lintr configuration and a CI entry point if linting is part of the stabilization bar.

Title: Rd example sections are absent
Evidence: No \examples{} section was detected across man/*.Rd files.
Impact: Example execution cannot contribute runtime evidence during check-like validation.
Recommended_next_action: Add minimal runnable examples for the core public API surface.

Title: CI matrix does not exercise macOS
Evidence: The only detected CI OS targets are: {ubuntu-latest, windows-latest}.
Impact: Platform-specific issues may remain hidden until later release-stage validation.
Recommended_next_action: Consider adding a macOS lane if cross-platform behavior is part of the stabilization target.

Title: CI check configuration skips manual and vignette validation
Evidence: R-CMD-check workflow passes --no-manual and ignores vignette building/checking.
Impact: Release-like validation is narrower than a full CRAN-style check surface.
Recommended_next_action: Retain fast CI if needed, but add an explicit full-check path before release hardening.

Title: Reference scaffold still contains TODO placeholders
Evidence: inst/REFERENCES.md contains unresolved DOI/URL TODO placeholders.
Impact: Citation completeness remains below a publication-ready documentation bar.
Recommended_next_action: Replace placeholder reference fields with finalized citations during later documentation work.

Title: Many internal metric ids have no obvious dedicated test file
Evidence: Direct filename-level evidence for dedicated tests is unclear for 26 metric ids, including: br2, dr, kge, kgekm, kgelf, kgenp, mape, md
Impact: Coverage may rely on broad batch tests, which makes gap analysis less precise during stabilization.
Recommended_next_action: Map metric ids to explicit tests or document which batch files provide intended coverage.

Title: CI does not upload diagnostic artifacts
Evidence: No upload-artifact step was detected in .github/workflows.
Impact: Troubleshooting failed checks may require rerunning jobs rather than inspecting retained artifacts.
Recommended_next_action: Consider uploading check logs or coverage outputs when deeper CI forensics become necessary.
